en.extract:
The striped wren-babbler (Kenopia striata) is a species of bird in the family Pellorneidae. It is monotypic within the genus Kenopia. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, and Thailand. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical swampland. It is threatened by habitat loss.


References

Collar, N. J. & Robson, C. 2007. Family Timaliidae (Babblers) pp. 70 – 291 in; del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A. & Christie, D.A. eds. Handbook of the Birds of the World, Vol. 12. Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona.

The '''striped wren-babbler''' ('''''Kenopia striata''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pellorneidae]]. It is [[monotypic]] within the genus '''''Kenopia'''''.<ref name = "ITIS"/> It is found in [[Brunei]], [[Indonesia]], [[Malaysia]], and [[Thailand]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" /> Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical [[swampland]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" /> It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

zh.extract:
纹鹪鹛（学名：Kenopia striata），是雀眉科纹鹪鹛属的一种，且仅仅只有这一种。分布于文莱、泰国、印度尼西亚、马来西亚和新加坡（已绝灭）。该物种的保护状况被评为近危。
其种加词“striata”意为“有条纹的”。
纹鹪鹛的平均体重约为19.8克。栖息地包括亚热带或热带的湿润低地林和亚热带或热带的湿润山地林。


参考文献
